\index{RandomWeakTournament (class in randomDigraphs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:randomDigraphs.RandomWeakTournament}\pysiglinewithargsret{\strong{class }\code{randomDigraphs.}\bfcode{RandomWeakTournament}}{\emph{order=10}, \emph{ndigits=2}, \emph{hasIntegerValuation=False}, \emph{weaknessDegree=0.25}, \emph{seed=None}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[techDoc:digraphs.Digraph]{\emph{\code{digraphs.Digraph}}}}

Specialization of the general Digraph class for generating
temporary bipolar-valued weak tournaments
\begin{description}
\item[{\emph{Parameters}:}] \leavevmode\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
order = n \textgreater{} 0

\item {} 
weaknessDegree in {[}0.0,1.0{]}: proportion of indeterminate links (default = 0.25)

\item {} 
If hasIntegerValuation = True,
valuation domain = {[}-pow(10,ndigits); + pow(10,ndigits){]}
else valuation domain = {[}-1.0,1.0{]}

\item {} 
If seed != None, the random number generator is seeded

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{Random3ObjectivesPerformanceTableau (class in randomPerfTabs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.Random3ObjectivesPerformanceTableau}\pysiglinewithargsret{\strong{class }\code{randomPerfTabs.}\bfcode{Random3ObjectivesPerformanceTableau}}{\emph{numberOfActions=20}, \emph{numberOfCriteria=13}, \emph{weightDistribution='equiobjectives'}, \emph{weightScale=None}, \emph{integerWeights=True}, \emph{OrdinalScales=False}, \emph{commonScale=None}, \emph{commonThresholds=None}, \emph{commonMode=None}, \emph{valueDigits=2}, \emph{vetoProbability=0.5}, \emph{missingProbability=0.05}, \emph{seed=None}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau]{\emph{\code{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Specialization of the PerformanceTableau
for 3 objectives: \emph{Eco}, \emph{Soc} and \emph{Env}.

Each decision action is qualified at random as weak (-), fair (\textasciitilde{}) or good (+)
on each of the three objectives.
\begin{description}
\item[{Generator arguments:}] \leavevmode\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
numberOf Actions := 20 (default)

\item {} 
number of Criteria := 13 (default)

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{weightDistribution := `equiobjectives' (default)}] \leavevmode
\begin{DUlineblock}{0em}
\item[] `equisignificant' (weights set all to 1)
\item[] `random' (in the range 1 to numberOfCriteria)
\end{DUlineblock}

\end{description}

\item {} 
weightScale := {[}1,numerOfCriteria{]} (random default)

\item {} 
integerWeights := True (default) / False

\item {} 
OrdinalScales := True / False (default), if True commonScale is set to (0,10)

\item {} 
commonScale := (0.0, 100.0) (default if OrdinalScales == False)

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{commonThresholds := {[}(1.0,0.0),(2.001,0.0),(8.001,0.0){]} if OrdinalScales == True, otherwise}] \leavevmode
\begin{DUlineblock}{0em}
\item[] {[}(0.10001*span,0.0),(0.20001*span,0.0),(0.80001*span,0.0){]} with span = commonScale{[}1{]} - commonScale{[}0{]}.
\end{DUlineblock}

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{commonMode := {[}'triangular','variable',0.50{]} (default), A constant mode may be provided.}] \leavevmode
\begin{DUlineblock}{0em}
\item[] {[}'uniform','variable',None{]}, a constant range may be provided.
\item[] {[}'beta','variable',None{]} (three alpha, beta combinations (5.8661,2.62203)
\item[]
\begin{DUlineblock}{\DUlineblockindent}
\item[] chosen by default for `good', `fair' and `weak' evaluations. Constant parameters may be provided.
\end{DUlineblock}
\end{DUlineblock}

\end{description}

\item {} 
valueDigits := 2 (default, for cardinal scales only)

\item {} 
vetoProbability := x in {]}0.0-1.0{[} (0.5 default), probability that a cardinal criterion shows a veto preference discrimination threshold.

\item {} 
Debug := True / False (default)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\index{RandomCBPerformanceTableau (class in randomPerfTabs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.RandomCBPerformanceTableau}\pysiglinewithargsret{\strong{class }\code{randomPerfTabs.}\bfcode{RandomCBPerformanceTableau}}{\emph{numberOfActions=13}, \emph{numberOfCriteria=7}, \emph{weightDistribution='equiobjectives'}, \emph{weightScale=None}, \emph{integerWeights=True}, \emph{commonScale=None}, \emph{commonThresholds=None}, \emph{commonPercentiles=None}, \emph{samplingSize=100000}, \emph{commonMode=None}, \emph{valueDigits=2}, \emph{missingDataProbability=0.0}, \emph{seed=None}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrCores=None}, \emph{Debug=False}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau]{\emph{\code{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Full automatic generation of random
Cost versus Benefit oriented performance tableaux.

Parameters:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
If numberOfActions == None, a uniform random number between 10 and 31 of cheap, neutral or advantageous actions (equal 1/3 probability each type) actions is instantiated

\item {} 
If numberOfCriteria == None, a uniform random number between 5 and 21 of cost or benefit criteria (1/3 respectively 2/3 probability) is instantiated

\item {} 
weightDistribution := \{`equiobjectives'\textbar{}'fixed'\textbar{}'random'\textbar{}'equisignificant' (default = `equisignificant')\}

\item {} 
default weightScale for `random' weightDistribution is 1 - numberOfCriteria

\item {} 
commonScale parameter is obsolete. The scale of cost criteria is cardinal or ordinal (0-10) with proabailities 1/4 respectively 3/4, whereas the scale of benefit criteria is ordinal or cardinal with probabilities 2/3, respectively 1/3.

\item {} 
All cardinal criteria are evaluated with decimals between 0.0 and 100.0 wheras all ordinal criteria are evaluated with integers between 0 and 10.

\item {} 
commonThresholds is obsolete. Preference discrimination is specified as percentiles of concerned performance differences (see below).

\item {} 
CommonPercentiles = \{`ind':0.05, `pref':0.10, {[}'weakveto':0.90,{]} `veto':`95\} are expressed in centiless (reversed for vetoes) and only concern cardinal criteria.

\end{itemize}

\begin{notice}{warning}{Warning:}
Minimal number of decision actions required is 3 !
\end{notice}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{RandomCoalitionsPerformanceTableau (class in randomPerfTabs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.RandomCoalitionsPerformanceTableau}\pysiglinewithargsret{\strong{class }\code{randomPerfTabs.}\bfcode{RandomCoalitionsPerformanceTableau}}{\emph{numberOfActions=None}, \emph{numberOfCriteria=None}, \emph{weightDistribution=None}, \emph{weightScale=None}, \emph{integerWeights=True}, \emph{commonScale=None}, \emph{commonThresholds=None}, \emph{commonMode=None}, \emph{valueDigits=2}, \emph{Coalitions=True}, \emph{VariableGenerators=True}, \emph{OrdinalScales=False}, \emph{Debug=False}, \emph{RandomCoalitions=False}, \emph{vetoProbability=None}, \emph{seed=None}, \emph{Electre3=True}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau]{\emph{\code{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Full automatic generation of performance tableaux with random coalitions of criteria
\begin{description}
\item[{Parameters:}] \leavevmode
\begin{DUlineblock}{0em}
\item[] numberOf Actions := 20 (default)
\item[] number of Criteria := 13 (default)
\item[] weightDistribution := `equisignificant' (default with all weights = 1.0), `random', `fixed' (default w\_1 = numberOfCriteria-1, w\_\{i!=1\} = 1
\item[] weightScale := {[}1,numerOfCriteria{]} (random default), {[}w\_1, w\_\{i!=1{]} (fixed)
\item[] integerWeights := True (default) / False
\item[] commonScale := (0.0, 100.0) (default)
\item[] commonThresholds := {[}(1.0,0.0),(2.001,0.0),(8.001,0.0){]} if OrdinalSacles, {[}(0.10001*span,0),(0.20001*span,0.0),(0.80001*span,0.0){]} with span = commonScale{[}1{]} - commonScale{[}0{]}.
\item[] commonMode := {[}'triangular',50.0,0.50{]} (default), {[}'uniform',None,None{]}, {[}'beta', None,None{]} (three alpha, beta combinations (5.8661,2.62203) chosen by default for high(`+'), medium (`\textasciitilde{}') and low (`-`) evaluations.
\item[] valueDigits := 2 (default, for cardinal scales only)
\item[] Coalitions := True (default)/False, three coalitions if True
\item[] VariableGenerators := True (default) / False, variable high(`+'), medium (`\textasciitilde{}') or low (`-`) law generated evaluations.
\item[] OrdinalScales := True / False (default)
\item[] Debug := True / False (default)
\item[] RandomCoalitions = True / False (default) zero or more than three coalitions if Coalitions == False.
\item[] vetoProbability := x in {]}0.0-1.0{[} / None (default), probability that a cardinal criterion shows a veto preference discrimination threshold.
\item[] Electre3 := True (default) / False, no weakveto if True (obsolete)
\end{DUlineblock}

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{RandomPerformanceTableau (class in randomPerfTabs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.RandomPerformanceTableau}\pysiglinewithargsret{\strong{class }\code{randomPerfTabs.}\bfcode{RandomPerformanceTableau}}{\emph{numberOfActions=13}, \emph{numberOfCriteria=7}, \emph{weightDistribution='equisignificant'}, \emph{weightScale=None}, \emph{integerWeights=True}, \emph{commonScale=(0.0}, \emph{100.0)}, \emph{commonThresholds=((10.0}, \emph{0.0)}, \emph{(20.0}, \emph{0.0)}, \emph{(80.0}, \emph{0.0))}, \emph{commonMode=None}, \emph{valueDigits=2}, \emph{missingDataProbability=0.0}, \emph{seed=None}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau]{\emph{\code{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Specialization of the PerformanceTableau class for generating a temporary
random performance tableau.
\begin{description}
\item[{Parameters:}] \leavevmode\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
numberOfActions := nbr of decision actions.

\item {} 
numberOfCriteria := number performance criteria.

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{weightDistribution := `random' (default) \textbar{} `fixed' \textbar{} `equisignificant'.}] \leavevmode
\begin{DUlineblock}{0em}
\item[] If `random', weights are uniformly selected randomly
\item[] form the given weight scale;
\item[] If `fixed', the weightScale must provided a corresponding weights
\item[] distribution;
\item[] If `equisignificant', all criterion weights are put to unity.
\end{DUlineblock}

\end{description}

\item {} 
weightScale := {[}Min,Max{]} (default ={[}1,numberOfCriteria{]}.

\item {} 
IntegerWeights := True (default) \textbar{} False (normalized to proportions of 1.0).

\item {} 
commonScale := {[}Min;Max{]}; common performance measuring scales (default = {[}0;100{]})

\item {} 
commonThresholds := {[}(q0,q1),(p0,p1),(v0,v1){]}; common indifference(q), preference (p)
and considerable performance difference discrimination thresholds.

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{commonMode := common random distribution of random performance measurements:}] \leavevmode
\begin{DUlineblock}{0em}
\item[] (`uniform',Min,Max), uniformly distributed between min and max values. 
\item[] (`normal',mu,sigma), truncated Gaussion distribution. 
\item[] (`triangular',mode,repartition), generalized triangular distribution 
\item[] (`beta',alpha,beta).
\end{DUlineblock}

\end{description}

\item {} 
valueDigits := \textless{}integer\textgreater{}, precision of performance measurements
(2 decimal digits by default).

\end{itemize}

\item[{Code example::}] \leavevmode
\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{randomPerfTabs} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{RandomPerformanceTableau}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{t} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{RandomPerformanceTableau}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{numberOfActions}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{numberOfCriteria}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{seed}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{t}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{actions}
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}a1\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}comment\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}RandomPerformanceTableau() generated.\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}name\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}random decision action\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{},}
\PYG{g+go}{     \PYGZsq{}a2\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}comment\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}RandomPerformanceTableau() generated.\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}name\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}random decision action\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{},}
\PYG{g+go}{     \PYGZsq{}a3\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}comment\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}RandomPerformanceTableau() generated.\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}name\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}random decision action\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{}\PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{t}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{criteria}
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}g1\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}thresholds\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}ind\PYGZsq{} : (Decimal(\PYGZsq{}10.0\PYGZsq{}), Decimal(\PYGZsq{}0.0\PYGZsq{})),}
\PYG{g+go}{                           \PYGZsq{}veto\PYGZsq{}: (Decimal(\PYGZsq{}80.0\PYGZsq{}), Decimal(\PYGZsq{}0.0\PYGZsq{})),}
\PYG{g+go}{                           \PYGZsq{}pref\PYGZsq{}: (Decimal(\PYGZsq{}20.0\PYGZsq{}), Decimal(\PYGZsq{}0.0\PYGZsq{}))\PYGZcb{},}
\PYG{g+go}{            \PYGZsq{}scale\PYGZsq{}: [0.0, 100.0],}
\PYG{g+go}{            \PYGZsq{}weight\PYGZsq{}: Decimal(\PYGZsq{}1\PYGZsq{}),}
\PYG{g+go}{            \PYGZsq{}name\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}digraphs.RandomPerformanceTableau() instance\PYGZsq{},}
\PYG{g+go}{            \PYGZsq{}comment\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}Arguments: ; weightDistribution=random;}
\PYG{g+go}{                weightScale=(1, 1); commonMode=None\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{}\PYGZcb{}}
\end{Verbatim}

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{t}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{evaluation}
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}g01\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}a01\PYGZsq{}: Decimal(\PYGZsq{}45.95\PYGZsq{}),}
\PYG{g+go}{             \PYGZsq{}a02\PYGZsq{}: Decimal(\PYGZsq{}95.17\PYGZsq{}),}
\PYG{g+go}{             \PYGZsq{}a03\PYGZsq{}: Decimal(\PYGZsq{}17.47\PYGZsq{})}
\PYG{g+go}{            \PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZcb{}}
\end{Verbatim}

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{RandomRankPerformanceTableau (class in randomPerfTabs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.RandomRankPerformanceTableau}\pysiglinewithargsret{\strong{class }\code{randomPerfTabs.}\bfcode{RandomRankPerformanceTableau}}{\emph{numberOfActions=13}, \emph{numberOfCriteria=7}, \emph{weightDistribution='equisignificant'}, \emph{weightScale=None}, \emph{commonThresholds=None}, \emph{integerWeights=True}, \emph{seed=None}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau]{\emph{\code{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Specialization of the PerformanceTableau class for generating a temporary
random performance tableau.

Random generator for multiple criteria ranked (without ties) performances of a
given number of decision actions. On each criterion,
all decision actions are hence lineraly ordered. The RandomRankPerformanceTableau class is
matching the RandomLinearVotingProfiles class (see the votingDigraphs module)
\begin{description}
\item[{\emph{Parameters}:}] \leavevmode\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
number of actions,

\item {} 
number of performance criteria,

\item {} 
weightDistribution := equisignificant \textbar{} random (default, see RandomPerformanceTableau)

\item {} 
weightScale := (1, 1 \textbar{} numberOfCriteria (default when random))

\item {} 
integerWeights := Boolean (True = default)

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{commonThresholds (default) := \{}] \leavevmode
\begin{DUlineblock}{0em}
\item[] `ind':(0,0),
\item[] `pref':(1,0),
\item[] `veto':(numberOfActions,0)
\item[] \} (default)
\end{DUlineblock}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{RandomS3PerformanceTableau (class in randomPerfTabs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.RandomS3PerformanceTableau}\pysiglinewithargsret{\strong{class }\code{randomPerfTabs.}\bfcode{RandomS3PerformanceTableau}}{\emph{numberOfActions=None}, \emph{numberOfCriteria=None}, \emph{weightDistribution=None}, \emph{weightScale=None}, \emph{integerWeights=True}, \emph{commonScale=None}, \emph{commonThresholds=None}, \emph{commonMode=None}, \emph{valueDigits=2}, \emph{Coalitions=True}, \emph{VariableGenerators=True}, \emph{OrdinalScales=False}, \emph{Debug=False}, \emph{RandomCoalitions=False}, \emph{vetoProbability=None}, \emph{seed=None}, \emph{Electre3=True}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[techDoc:randomPerfTabs.RandomCoalitionsPerformanceTableau]{\emph{\code{randomPerfTabs.RandomCoalitionsPerformanceTableau}}}}

Obsolete dummy class for backports.

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsection{bigOutrankingDigraphs module}
\label{techDoc:bigoutrankingdigraphs-label}\label{techDoc:module-bigOutrankingDigraphs}\label{techDoc:bigoutrankingdigraphs-module}\index{bigOutrankingDigraphs (module)}\index{BigDigraph (class in bigOutrankingDigraphs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph}\pysigline{\strong{class }\code{bigOutrankingDigraphs.}\bfcode{BigDigraph}}
Bases: \href{http://docs.python.org/library/functions.html\#object}{\code{object}}

abstract root class for linearly decomposed big digraphs (order \textgreater{} 1000)
using multiprocessing ressources.
\index{computeDecompositionSummaryStatistics() (bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph.computeDecompositionSummaryStatistics}\pysiglinewithargsret{\bfcode{computeDecompositionSummaryStatistics}}{}{}
Returns the summary of the distribution of the length of
the components as follows:

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
summary = \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}max\PYGZsq{}: maxLength,
           \PYGZsq{}median\PYGZsq{}:medianLength,
           \PYGZsq{}mean\PYGZsq{}:meanLength,
           \PYGZsq{}stdev\PYGZsq{}: stdLength,
           \PYGZsq{}fillrate\PYGZsq{}: fillrate,
                      (see computeFillRate()\PYGZcb{}
\end{Verbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeFillRate() (bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph.computeFillRate}\pysiglinewithargsret{\bfcode{computeFillRate}}{}{}
Renders the sum of the squares (without diagonal) of the orders of the component's subgraphs
over the square (without diagonal) of the big digraph order.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeOrdinalCorrelation() (bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph.computeOrdinalCorrelation}\pysiglinewithargsret{\bfcode{computeOrdinalCorrelation}}{\emph{other}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Renders the ordinal correlation K of a BigDigraph instance
when compared with a given compatible (same actions set) other Digraph or
BigDigraph instance.

K = sum\_\{x != y\} {[} min( max(-self.relation(x,y)),other.relation(x,y), max(self.relation(x,y),-other.relation(x,y)) {]}

K /= sum\_\{x!=y\} {[} min(abs(self.relation(x,y),abs(other.relation(x,y)) {]}

\begin{notice}{note}{Note:}
The global outranking relation of BigDigraph instances is contructed on the fly
from the ordered dictionary of the components.

Renders a tuple with at position 0 the actual bipolar correlation index
and in position 1 the minimal determination level D of self and
the other relation.

D = sum\_\{x != y\} min(abs(self.relation(x,y)),abs(other.relation(x,y)) / n(n-1)

where n is the number of actions considered.

The correlation index with a completely indeterminate relation
is by convention 0.0 at determination level 0.0 .
\end{notice}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{ordering2Preorder() (bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph.ordering2Preorder}\pysiglinewithargsret{\bfcode{ordering2Preorder}}{\emph{ordering}}{}
Renders a preordering (a list of list) of a linar order (worst to best) of decision actions in increasing preference direction.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{ranking2Preorder() (bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph.ranking2Preorder}\pysiglinewithargsret{\bfcode{ranking2Preorder}}{\emph{ranking}}{}
Renders a preordering (a list of list) of a ranking (best to worst) of decision actions in increasing preference direction.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{recodeValuation() (bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph.recodeValuation}\pysiglinewithargsret{\bfcode{recodeValuation}}{\emph{newMin=-1}, \emph{newMax=1}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Specialization for recoding the valuation of all the partial digraphs and the component relation.
By default the valuation domain is normalized to {[}-1;1{]}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{relation() (bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph.relation}\pysiglinewithargsret{\bfcode{relation}}{\emph{x}, \emph{y}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Dynamic construction of the global outranking characteristic function \emph{r(x S y)}.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{showDecomposition() (bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph.showDecomposition}\pysiglinewithargsret{\bfcode{showDecomposition}}{\emph{direction='decreasing'}}{}
Prints on the console the decomposition structure of the sparse outranking digraph instance
in \emph{decreasing} (default) or \emph{increasing} preference direction.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{showRelationMap() (bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph.showRelationMap}\pysiglinewithargsret{\bfcode{showRelationMap}}{\emph{symbols=None}}{}
Prints on the console, in text map format, the location of
the diagonal outranking components of the big outranking digraph.
\begin{description}
\item[{By default, symbols = \{`max':'┬','positive': `+', `median': ` `,}] \leavevmode
`negative': `-`, `min': `┴'\}

\end{description}

The default ordering of the output is following the quantiles sorted boosted net flows ranking rule
from best to worst actions. Further available ranking rules are Kohler's (rankingRule=''kohler'')
and Tideman's ranked pairs rule (rankingRule=''rankedPairs'').

Example:

\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{bigOutrankingDigraphs} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{o}{*}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{t} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{RandomCBPerformanceTableau}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{numberOfActions}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{50}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{seed}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{bg} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{BigOutrankingDigraphMP}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{t}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{quantiles}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{minimalComponentSize}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{bg}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{*\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{} show short \PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}*}
\PYG{g+go}{Instance name     : randomCBperftab\PYGZus{}mp}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZsh{} Actions         : 50}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZsh{} Criteria        : 7}
\PYG{g+go}{Sorting by        : 10\PYGZhy{}Tiling}
\PYG{g+go}{Ordering strategy : average}
\PYG{g+go}{Ranking Rule      : Copeland}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZsh{} Components      : 7}
\PYG{g+go}{Minimal size      : 5}
\PYG{g+go}{Maximal size      : 13}
\PYG{g+go}{Median size       : 6}
\PYG{g+go}{fill rate         : 16.898\PYGZpc{}}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}  Constructor run times (in sec.) \PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}}
\PYG{g+go}{Total time        : 0.08494}
\PYG{g+go}{QuantilesSorting  : 0.04339}
\PYG{g+go}{Preordering       : 0.00034}
\PYG{g+go}{Decomposing       : 0.03989}
\PYG{g+go}{Ordering          : 0.00024}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZlt{}class \PYGZsq{}bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraphMP\PYGZsq{}\PYGZgt{} instance         }
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{bg}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{showRelationMap}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{ ┬+++┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬}
\PYG{g+go}{┴ ++┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬┬}

\index{BigOutrankingDigraph (class in bigOutrankingDigraphs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraph}\pysigline{\strong{class }\code{bigOutrankingDigraphs.}\bfcode{BigOutrankingDigraph}}
Bases: {\hyperref[techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph]{\emph{\code{bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigDigraph}}}}

Abstract root class  for the multiprocessing implementation of big outranking digraphs.

The big outranking digraph instance is decomposed with a q-tiling sort into a partition
of quantile equivalence classes which are linearly ordered by average quantile limits (default).

With each quantile equivalence class is associated a BipolarOutrankingDigraph object
which is restricted to the decision actions gathered in this quantile equivalence class.

By default, the number of quantiles q is set to a tenth of the number of decision actions,
ie q = order//10.

For other parameters settings, see the corresponding {\hyperref[techDoc:sortingDigraphs.QuantilesSortingDigraph]{\emph{\code{sortingDigraphs.QuantilesSortingDigraph}}}} class.
\index{computeBoostedOrdering() (bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraph.computeBoostedOrdering}\pysiglinewithargsret{\bfcode{computeBoostedOrdering}}{\emph{orderingRule='Copeland'}}{}
Renders an ordred list of decision actions ranked in
increasing preference direction following by default the Copeland rule
on each component.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeBoostedRanking() (bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraph.computeBoostedRanking}\pysiglinewithargsret{\bfcode{computeBoostedRanking}}{\emph{rankingRule='Copeland'}}{}
Renders an ordred list of decision actions ranked in
decreasing preference direction following the net flows rule
on each component.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeCriterion2RankingCorrelation() (bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraph.computeCriterion2RankingCorrelation}\pysiglinewithargsret{\bfcode{computeCriterion2RankingCorrelation}}{\emph{criterion}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrOfCPUs=None}, \emph{Debug=False}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}
Renders the ordinal correlation coefficient between
the global outranking and the marginal criterion relation.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeMarginalVersusGlobalOutrankingCorrelations() (bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraph.computeMarginalVersusGlobalOutrankingCorrelations}\pysiglinewithargsret{\bfcode{computeMarginalVersusGlobalOutrankingCorrelations}}{\emph{Sorted=True}, \emph{ValuedCorrelation=False}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrCores=None}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}
Method for computing correlations between each individual criterion relation with the corresponding
global outranking relation.

Returns a list of tuples (correlation,criterionKey) sorted by default in decreasing order of the correlation.

If Threading is True, a multiprocessing Pool class is used with a parallel equivalent of the built-in map function.

If nbrCores is not set, the os.cpu\_count() function is used to determine the number of
available cores.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{BigOutrankingDigraphMP (class in bigOutrankingDigraphs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraphMP}\pysiglinewithargsret{\strong{class }\code{bigOutrankingDigraphs.}\bfcode{BigOutrankingDigraphMP}}{\emph{argPerfTab=None}, \emph{quantiles=None}, \emph{quantilesOrderingStrategy='average'}, \emph{LowerClosed=True}, \emph{componentRankingRule='Copeland'}, \emph{minimalComponentSize=None}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrOfCPUs=None}, \emph{nbrOfThreads=None}, \emph{save2File=None}, \emph{CopyPerfTab=True}, \emph{Comments=False}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraph]{\emph{\code{bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraph}}}}, {\hyperref[techDoc:weakOrders.QuantilesRankingDigraph]{\emph{\code{weakOrders.QuantilesRankingDigraph}}}}, {\hyperref[techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau]{\emph{\code{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Multiprocessing implementation of the abstract BipolarOutrankingDigraph class
for large instances (order \textgreater{} 1000)

The outranking digraph is decomposed with a q-tiling sort into a partition of
quantile equivalence classes, which are linearly ordred by average quantile limits (default).

Wich each quantile equivalence class is associated a BipolarOutrankingDigraph object
which is by default restricted to a Digraph instance;
the corresponding restricted performance tableau being dropped.

By default, q is set to a tenth of the number of decision actions,
ie q = order//10.

For other parameters settings, see the corresponding QuantilesSortingDigraph class.
\index{computeActionCategories() (bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraphMP method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{techDoc:bigOutrankingDigraphs.BigOutrankingDigraphMP.computeActionCategories}\pysiglinewithargsret{\bfcode{computeActionCategories}}{\emph{action}, \emph{Show=False}, \emph{Debug=False}, \emph{Comments=False}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrOfCPUs=None}}{}
Renders the union of categories in which the given action is sorted positively or null into.
Returns a tuple : action, lowest category key, highest category key, membership credibility !

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}
